ddls:
CREATE TABLE "films" (
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each film */,
  "imdb_id" TEXT /* IMDB identifier for the film */,
  "original_title" TEXT /* Original title of the film */,
  "popularity" REAL /* Popularity metric of the film */,
  "budget" REAL /* Total budget allocated for the film */,
  "revenue" REAL /* Total revenue generated by the film */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date of film release */,
  "runtime" INTEGER /* Runtime of the film in minutes */,
  "overview" TEXT /* A brief overview of the film's plot */,
  "tagline" TEXT /* Tagline associated with the film */,
  "director_name" TEXT /* Name of the film's director */,
  "production_country" TEXT /* Country where the film was produced */,
  "language" TEXT /* Original language of the film */,
  "certification" TEXT /* Age rating or certification of the film */,
  "trailer_path" TEXT /* File path to the film's trailer */,
  "poster_path" TEXT /* File path to the film's poster */,
  "imdb_rating" REAL /* Rating of the film on IMDB */,
  "imdb_votes" INTEGER /* Number of votes the film has received on IMDB */,
  "box_office_usa" REAL /* Box office revenue in the USA */,
  "box_office_worldwide" REAL /* Worldwide box office revenue */,
  "streaming_services" TEXT /* List of streaming services where the film is available */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "casts" (
  "cast_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cast entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film associated with the cast */,
  "actor_name" TEXT /* Full name of the actor */,
  "character_name" TEXT /* Character name portrayed in the film */,
  "actor_gender" TEXT /* Gender of the actor */,
  "actor_birthdate" TEXT /* Birthdate of the actor */,
  "actor_bio" TEXT /* Biography of the actor */,
  "character_gender" TEXT /* Gender of the character */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cast_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_casts_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "production_companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each production company entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film associated with the production company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the production company */,
  "company_country" TEXT /* Country where the production company is based */,
  "company_founded_year" INTEGER /* Year the production company was founded */,
  "company_description" TEXT /* Description of the production company */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_production_companies_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film associated with the genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre (e.g., Comedy, Action) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_genres_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "rating" INTEGER /* Rating given by the user */,
  "review" TEXT /* Review text provided by the user */,
  "rating_date" TEXT /* Date when the rating was given */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_ratings_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_ratings_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Name of the user */,
  "user_email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "user_role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., analyst, marketer, researcher) */,
  "user_bio" TEXT /* Biography of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "streaming_data" (
  "stream_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each streaming entry */,
  "film_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the film */,
  "streaming_service" TEXT /* Name of the streaming service */,
  "viewership" INTEGER /* Number of viewers on the streaming service */,
  "streaming_country" TEXT /* Country where the streaming data is collected */,
  "streaming_date" TEXT /* Date when the streaming data was recorded */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stream_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_streaming_data_film_id FOREIGN KEY ("film_id") REFERENCES films ("film_id")
)
